# terrible_amazon_aws_rds_instance_snapshot (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `db_snapshot_identifier` (String) The snapshot to manage.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `copy_tags` (Boolean) Whether to copy all tags from `source_db_snapshot_identifier` to `db_instance_identifier`.
- `db_instance_identifier` (String) Database instance identifier. Required when creating a snapshot.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `source_db_snapshot_identifier` (String) The identifier of the source DB snapshot. Required when copying a snapshot. If the source snapshot is in the same AWS region as the copy, specify the snapshot's identifier. If the source snapshot is in a different AWS region as the copy, specify the snapshot's ARN.
- `source_region` (String) The region that contains the snapshot to be copied.
- `state` (String) Specify the desired state of the snapshot.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `wait` (Boolean) Whether or not to wait for snapshot creation or deletion.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) how long before wait gives up, in seconds.

### Read-Only

- `allocated_storage` (Number) How much storage is allocated in GB.
- `availability_zone` (String) Availability zone of the database from which the snapshot was created.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `db_snapshot_arn` (String) Amazon Resource Name for the snapshot.
- `dbi_resource_id` (String) The identifier for the source DB instance, which can't be changed and which is unique to an AWS Region.
- `encrypted` (Boolean) Whether the snapshot is encrypted.
- `engine` (String) Engine of the database from which the snapshot was created.
- `engine_version` (String) Version of the database from which the snapshot was created.
- `iam_database_authentication_enabled` (Boolean) Whether IAM database authentication is enabled.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `instance_create_time` (String) Creation time of the instance from which the snapshot was created.
- `license_model` (String) License model of the database.
- `master_username` (String) Master username of the database.
- `option_group_name` (String) Option group of the database.
- `percent_progress` (Number) How much progress has been made taking the snapshot. Will be 100 for an available snapshot.
- `port` (Number) Port on which the database is listening.
- `processor_features` (String) List of processor features of the database.
- `snapshot_create_time` (String) Creation time of the snapshot.
- `snapshot_type` (String) How the snapshot was created (always manual for this module!).
- `status` (String) Status of the snapshot.
- `storage_type` (String) Storage type of the database.
- `vpc_id` (String) ID of the VPC in which the DB lives.
